data.body:
Goal:
Make Foolery expose its HTTP API in a user- and agent-friendly, idiomatic way. Foolery already serves /api/openapi.json and /api/docs; this work should improve discoverability and machine consumption so agents can reliably find API capabilities, schemas, examples, and base-url guidance without reading source code.

Verification:
1. Run `curl -sS http://localhost:3000/api/openapi.json` against a dev server and confirm stale grooming, registry, settings, terminal, plans, and core beat paths are present.
2. Open `http://localhost:3000/api/docs` and confirm the docs clearly show how agents should authenticate or connect locally, discover the base URL, resolve repositories, and use common workflows.
3. Run the documented agent discovery flow from a clean shell using curl or a small script and confirm it can find the OpenAPI spec, resolve a repo by name, and call at least one documented endpoint without source-code knowledge.

Constraints:
Keep the existing OpenAPI endpoint compatible. Prefer additive metadata, docs, examples, or well-known discovery endpoints over route-breaking changes. Do not introduce a PR workflow unless explicitly requested.
data.title: Host agent-friendly API resource
